title: Train-to-test density-ratio weighting with weighted calibration
summary: Your context already hints at shift: the train set has more drivers than test, and public/private splits may differ by year, race, and compound mix.
rationale: Your context already hints at shift: the train set has more drivers than test, and public/private splits may differ by year, race, and compound mix. Under covariate shift, standard CV can mislead, and weighting train rows toward the unlabeled test distribution is a well-studied fix.
implementation_hint: Fit a train-vs-test classifier on features available in both splits, convert its odds to clipped importance weights, and use those weights in model fitting or blend weighting. Evaluate with race-held-out IWCV-style folds, then apply a lightweight sigmoid calibrator on out-of-fold predictions using the same weights. Keep clipping aggressive to avoid variance blow-ups.
expected_effect: Most useful if leaderboard composition differs from training history; can improve public-score alignment even when local CV is flat.
risk: Poorly estimated weights can add variance and hurt both CV and LB, especially with many rare categories; weight clipping and a no-weight baseline are mandatory.
